# dart-inspector test fixtures

This directory holds the synthetic binaries consumed by `tests/modules/dart-inspector/`.
Both files are checked into git so tests stay reproducible without external assets;
both are tiny (≤ 100 KB) and regenerated by the scripts beside them.

## Files

| File | Purpose | Generated by |
|------|---------|--------------|
| `tiny-libapp.so` | 4 KB synthetic libapp with one of every default category, a triple-occurrence token, a cross-chunk straddle and a UTF-16LE entry | `build-tiny-libapp.ts` |
| `expected-strings.json` | Ground-truth offsets / category mapping for the fixture above (consumed by accuracy assertions) | `build-tiny-libapp.ts` |
| `redos-buffer.bin` | 100 KB of `0x61` ('a') — feeds the ReDoS integration tests | `build-redos-fixture.ts` |

## Regenerate

```bash
pnpm tsx tests/fixtures/dart-inspector/build-tiny-libapp.ts
pnpm tsx tests/fixtures/dart-inspector/build-redos-fixture.ts
```

The scripts are deterministic — running them twice produces byte-identical
output. If you change layout in `build-tiny-libapp.ts` you MUST regenerate
both `tiny-libapp.so` AND `expected-strings.json` together, then re-run
`pnpm test tests/modules/dart-inspector/` so the accuracy tests stay in sync.

## Why these constants

- **4 KB tiny-libapp**: large enough to straddle the 1024-byte chunk boundary
  (`DART_CHUNK_OVERLAP_BYTES` defaults to 64) yet still small enough to read
  in a single hex view when debugging.
- **100 KB ReDoS buffer**: large enough that an unbounded catastrophic pattern
  would burn seconds, ensuring `DART_REGEX_TIMEOUT_MS` (default 50 ms) trips
  cleanly. A smaller buffer would risk false-pass on machines that happen to
  finish quickly.
